title: Star Citizen's next update will switch focus to fixing its bug-riddled previous update, with bigger changes shunted to a later patch
take: Well, this is an interesting turn of events for Star Citizen! After hitting a mind-boggling $1 billion in funding, the team at CIG is now slamming the brakes on new features for their next update to focus almost entirely on squashing bugs. It seems the universe-resetting Alpha 4.8 introduced so many issues that even veteran players are fed up with workarounds, and new players are hitting major snags in their very first hour – yikes! While it means new content like the revamped Siege of Orison is delayed, maybe a massive bug-fixing effort is exactly what this ambitious space MMO needs to get its (many, many) systems flying smoothly.

title: Black Ops and Black Ops 2 come to PS5, but at a high price
take: Get ready to relive some classic Call of Duty action, PlayStation fans! Black Ops and Black Ops 2 have stealth-dropped onto PS4 and PS5, bringing the iconic campaigns, multiplayer, and zombies modes along for the ride. But hold up, these aren't the sparkly remasters we were all secretly hoping for; they're straight-up ports with no 4K, 120Hz, or FOV sliders. And at $39.99 a pop (plus season passes!), many are feeling the sting of nostalgia without the polish. The good news? No cross-play with the "hacked to death" Xbox versions, so maybe your K/D ratio will actually stand a chance!

title: The Weirdest Soccer Games of All Time
take: Forget your serious FIFA sims; IGN just dropped a list of the *weirdest* soccer games ever, and it's a glorious mess! We're talking about a Game Boy Advance platformer where David Beckham saves Soccer Island from evil monsters using his ball skills – yes, really, and it was made by a GTA co-creator! Then there's the truly petty "Peter Shilton’s Handball Maradona," a game born from a real-world grudge where you only play as a keeper defending scenarios. And don't even get us started on RedCard, a PS2/Xbox gem that encourages breaking opponents in half for super moves. If you thought you knew soccer games, prepare to have your mind blown by these bonkers beauties!

title: The Blood of Dawnwalker's most interesting mechanic took a bite out of my inner RPG completionist, who would've otherwise played hooky with a witch
take: The Blood of Dawnwalker is serving up some serious RPG choices, and we're totally here for it! This upcoming title isn't just another action-RPG with cool combat (though its For Honor-style directional blocks sound pretty sweet). The real game-changer is a tight 30-day/night deadline to save your family from a vampire lord, forcing you to make tough, time-sensitive decisions. Forget being a completionist; you'll have to choose between a witch's book club and saving lives. This urgency promises a truly unique and emotional gameplay experience where every choice actually matters.

title: Doom dev id Software reportedly halved following Xbox's mass layoffs
take: Get ready for some truly shocking news from the gaming world! id Software, the legendary studio that practically invented the first-person shooter genre with Doom, Quake, and Wolfenstein, has reportedly been cut in half by Xbox's recent mass layoffs. We're talking about a potential loss of nearly 100 employees, with alarming rumors suggesting that "most (if not all) coders" might be gone. This is a massive blow to a studio renowned for its incredible tech and groundbreaking in-house engine, especially after the huge success of Doom: The Dark Ages. It's tough to see a pioneer of gaming tech reduced to a possible support role, and it leaves us wondering about the future of those iconic franchises.

title: Steam Rules Must Change, Developer Says, After Players Praise Bitesize Game But Are Allowed to Refund It Anyway
take: Indie developer Zoroarts is calling out Steam's refund policy, and honestly, we get it! Their charming boat adventure, Paddle Paddle Paddle, is getting rave reviews, but players are finishing it in under two hours and still getting their money back. Imagine getting a "GREAT GAME" review only to see a 21% refund rate – that's over 55,000 refunds, folks! While Steam's two-hour window helps players avoid duds, it's clearly crushing creators of shorter, impactful experiences. Maybe a clearer "expected playtime" label is in order, because nobody wants a "too short" refund reason for a game they loved!

title: Counter-Strike 2 might be the biggest game on Steam, but Global Offensive is breaking player count records since it went standalone again
take: Counter-Strike 2 might dominate Steam, but get this: its predecessor, Global Offensive, is making a stunning comeback! Despite being un-merged from CS2 and not easily found on Steam, CS:GO just hit a post-re-release player peak of over 68,000 concurrent players. That's more players than blockbusters like Baldur's Gate 3 right now, which is absolutely wild for a 14-year-old game that isn't even actively promoted. It seems there’s more than enough room for multiple Counter-Strikes, proving that sometimes, older gems just refuse to fade away!

title: Pokémon Trading Card Game's awful scalping problem finally caught Nintendo's attention
take: It’s about time! Nintendo has finally acknowledged the absolutely wild scalping problem that’s making Pokémon Trading Card Game products impossible to get for regular players. Prices are through the roof, even at places like GameStop, thanks to bulk buyers and resellers. While Nintendo says they’re talking to The Pokémon Company about solutions like made-to-order sales and ID verification for online purchases, it feels a little like they’re passing the buck. Still, it’s good to know this issue is on their radar (and even their shareholders'!), so hopefully, we'll see more decisive action soon to make collecting fun again.

title: After Sony Kills PS5 Discs and GTA 6 is Just a Code in a Box, Xbox Is Using Halo: Campaign Evolved's Physical Disc as a Selling Point
take: Remember when a physical game disc in the box was, you know, normal? Well, Xbox is leaning into the nostalgia for Halo: Campaign Evolved, proudly proclaiming it’ll come on a real, honest-to-goodness disc! After Sony announced they’re ditching discs for new PS5 games and GTA 6 boxed editions are just download codes, Microsoft is making a big deal out of Master Chief actually being on a disc. It's a smart move to stand out, especially as they celebrate Halo’s anniversary, even if this remake is also hitting PlayStation. Talk about a full-circle moment for physical media!

title: The internet is agog at Captain America's hog
take: Well, folks, buckle up, because Marvel Rivals is dropping a Captain America skin that's causing quite a stir! Forget your usual heroic poses; this new "Seaside Sentinel" outfit is turning heads for a rather... anatomical reason. It seems NetEase has given Cap some seriously impressive beach bod physics, and yes, it's definitely noticeable and animated. We're living in a wild new era of character customization, where even superheroes are getting the full jiggle treatment!

title: Amazon Prime members can now stream 2025 superhero hit Dispatch
take: Hold onto your capes, Prime members! That critically acclaimed superhero gem, Dispatch, is now ready to stream on Amazon Luna. From the minds behind Telltale's narrative adventures, this game lets you step into the shoes of a former hero managing a crew of reformed villains – talk about a power trip! You'll be making tough calls and deciding which chaotic crusaders tackle which missions, all while enjoying sharp writing and slick direction. Plus, Amazon's stacking up the free PC games this month too, so it's a great time to be a Prime subscriber!

title: Millie Bobby Brown Reveals She Auditioned for X-23 in Logan
take: Millie Bobby Brown almost slashed her way into the Wolverine universe! Turns out, our favorite Eleven from Stranger Things auditioned for the intense role of X-23 in Logan back when she was just a kid, even screen-testing with Hugh Jackman himself. While she was "broken" not to get it, Dafne Keen absolutely nailed the part, even improvising in Spanish during her audition, adding so much depth to Laura. It makes you wonder how different things would have been, but Keen's iconic performance as X-23, especially seeing her pop up in the new Deadpool & Wolverine, just feels *right*.

title: The new Company of Heroes is a wave-based defense game that throws prospective generals straight into the action
take: Hold the line, commanders! Relic Entertainment just dropped a surprising new twist on the Company of Heroes universe with Final Stand, a wave-based defense game launching July 29th. Forget the deep RTS strategy for a moment; this standalone title throws you straight into escalating battles with bosses and roguelite progression, perfect for quick, intense sessions. Whether you’re a series veteran or a newcomer, the promise of dynamic battlefields and co-op action means you can jump right into the thrill of holding your ground against overwhelming odds. Plus, if you own CoH3, you snag a sweet 20% discount on this $30 Steam release!

title: Dragon Strikers gets surprise season 2 2027 release date by Disney Plus
take: Get ready to cheer, because Disney Plus's sleeper hit "Dragon Striker" is officially getting a second season in early 2027! This gorgeous blend of Eastern and Western animation, often compared to "Avatar: The Last Airbender" meets "Harry Potter" with a soccer twist, quietly captivated audiences after Disney barely marketed its initial release. Now, with a surprise announcement at Anime Expo, this fantasy sports series about farm boy Key Nagatatsu and his magical gorotama team has a real shot at the bigger audience it absolutely deserves. Go watch season one now so you're ready for more epic adventures and dragon-powered sports action!

title: Steven Spielberg to Produce Film Adaptation of YouTube Horror Series The Mandela Catalogue
take: Steven Spielberg producing a movie based on The Mandela Catalogue? Yes, please! The viral YouTube horror series, crafted by 22-year-old Alex Kister (who's directing the film!), is getting the big screen treatment with Amazon MGM. This "analog horror" hit, famous for its creepy doppelgangers and VHS aesthetic, started when Kister was just 17 and made a significant chunk on his phone – talk about a glow-up! It’s awesome to see Hollywood finally recognizing the incredible talent brewing on YouTube, with Backrooms and Obsession already proving that Gen Z is hungry for fresh, well-made horror. Get ready for some serious chills, gamers!
